info.title: CRMy API
info.description: The operational customer context backend for AI agents. MCP-native, PostgreSQL-backed, open source. Application endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <jwt-or-api-key>` except `/auth/register` and `/auth/login`; provider OAuth callbacks and inbound email webhooks use provider state or tenant-scoped HMAC signatures instead.

allow_duplicates.description: Skip duplicate check and create unconditionally. Use only after presenting candidates to the user.

if_exists.description: warn: return 409 with candidates when a duplicate is found. return_existing: silently return the best-matching existing record instead of creating.

expected_version.description: Optional optimistic concurrency guard. Pass the row_version from the last read to prevent overwriting newer state.

priority.description: Request urgency — affects notification format and SLA. Default: normal

sla_minutes.description: Minutes before SLA breach triggers escalation. Default: 1440 (24h)

escalate_to_id.description: Actor ID to escalate to if SLA breaches. Defaults to most senior active human.

handoff_snapshot_id.description: Agent handoff snapshot ID captured with agent_capture_handoff before submitting this review.

source_type.description: Where the evidence came from, such as activity, email, transcript, mcp, context_api, crm_sync, warehouse_sync, support, product_usage, slack, or research.

source_id.description: CRMy record ID for the source when available.

source_url.description: URL or deep link to the source system when available.

source_label.description: Human-readable source label, such as the meeting title or email subject.

speaker.description: Speaker or author for quoted evidence when known.

snippet.description: Short quote or excerpt supporting the claim.

captured_at.description: When CRMy captured or processed the source.

confidence.description: How strongly this evidence supports the claim.

rationale.description: Why this evidence supports the claim.

verified_at.description: When a human or policy last verified this evidence.

allow_similar.description: Bypass context convergence warnings and create a separate current entry even when similar or conflicting current context exists. Prefer context_supersede unless the new entry is intentionally distinct.

message.description: Notification message body. Required unless ai_generate is true.

channel_id.description: Messaging channel UUID (uses tenant default if omitted)

body_text.description: Plain-text email body. Supports {{variables}}. Required unless ai_generate is true.

require_approval.description: If true, creates a HITL request before sending

object_type.description: Entity type to update (defaults to triggered entity type)

object_id.description: Entity UUID or variable (defaults to triggered entity ID)

field.description: Field name to update, e.g. lifecycle_stage

type.description: Activity type, e.g. task, call, note

owner_id.description: UUID of the actor to assign as owner. Supports {{variables}}.

url.description: HTTPS endpoint to POST to when this action fires

secret.description: Optional shared secret for HMAC verification

sequence_id.description: UUID of the sequence to enroll the contact into

contact_id.description: Contact UUID override; auto-resolved from event subject if omitted

objective.description: Optional goal text for this enrollment

title.description: Review request title shown to the human reviewer. Supports {{variables}}.

instructions.description: Optional instructions for the reviewer — what to check or decide

object_type.description: CRMy object type, e.g. contact or opportunity

object_id.description: Optional CRMy object UUID or variable; defaults to the event subject when omitted

external_object.description: External object, table, or endpoint name

payload.description: JSON object or templated JSON string

max_runs_per_hour.description: Rate limit: maximum workflow runs allowed per hour

get.summary: Discover external objects or fields
List object mappings

post.summary: Create or update an object mapping

system_id.description: System connection that owns this mapping.

object_type.description: CRMy typed object that external records sync into. In 0.8, contact/account/opportunity/activity sync directly; use_case and context_entry mappings create reviewable conflicts until their typed adapters are available.

external_object.description: External CRM object, warehouse table, view, or approved stored procedure target.

external_id_field.description: External field used as the stable source record identifier.

watermark_field.description: Optional external updated-at or cursor field for incremental sync.

field_mapping.description: Map CRMy field names to external field names. Example: {"email":"properties.email"}.

readable_fields.description: External fields allowed to be read during sync. Empty means the adapter default is used.

writable_fields.description: External fields allowed for governed writeback. Empty means read-only writeback policy.

source_authority.description: Conflict policy for this mapping. External can update CRMy directly; CRMy/read-only/approval-required create conflicts instead of overwriting; bidirectional updates only when CRMy has not diverged from the last synced value.

writeback_mode.description: Approved external write mode. Warehouses only allow append_event, mapped_upsert, or stored_procedure.

writeback_config.description: Admin-defined writeback settings such as sql_template, table name, procedure name, or parameter_order.

allow_source_loop.description: Allow sync-originated events to write back to the same source. Disabled by default to prevent loops.

is_active.description: Disable to pause this mapping without deleting sync history.

post.summary: Run a system sync that emits normal CRMy events
